---
title: "LRPITT16: Modality contrast — taught expressive (TE) vs taught receptive (TR) vocabulary"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The generalisation contrasts [LRPITT15](../lrp-rli-itt-015) (expressive: TE vs UE) and
[LRPITT15b](../lrp-rli-itt-115) (receptive: TR vs UR) each compare taught vs not-taught
*within* a modality. Neither asks whether the intervention moved the directly-taught words
more in the **expressive** modality than in the **receptive** modality. LRPITT16 supplies that
missing contrast: the two Block 1 *taught* outcomes — expressive taught target words $TE$ and
receptive taught target words $TR$ — are fitted together as factorised marginals over the randomised window, and the
headline quantity is the difference in probability-scale intervention benefit, $\mathrm{AME}_{TE} - \mathrm{AME}_{TR}$.

A positive contrast means the intervention increased expected proportion correct more for taught expressive words than for taught receptive words. This is a modality contrast, not a taught-versus-untaught generalisation contrast. The conditional logit-coefficient difference is retained as a secondary summary.

## Design

Two outcome-specific Beta-Binomial marginals are fitted in one graph over the randomised phase-0 window ($G = 1$ intervention, $G = 0$ wait-list), with own baseline and linear age but no cross-baselines. The LKJ residual-correlation block is **off**, so the model does not estimate within-child posterior covariance and the contrast uncertainty is not guaranteed to be conservative. The report uses a common proportion-correct risk-difference scale but treats the result as an exploratory factorised sensitivity, not calibrated paired inference; confirmation requires a paired child-level randomisation-inference/permutation analysis, bootstrap, sandwich, or defensible dependence-model analysis. PSIS-LOO leaves out a child, not a child-outcome cell.

{{< include _partials/_results_joint.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
